title: Library Assignment Order
match_hints: available books in the library
issued books
required books
optimal sequence of studentIDs
if it is not possible output -1
notes: Choose the smallest studentID when multiple students can finish.
A student can finish when current available books cover required minus issued.
After finishing, the student returns all currently issued books.

title: Case-Insensitive Substring Count
match_hints: countOccur(parent, sub)
disregard the case of the letters
number of occurrences of the second string in the first string
if no occurrence of Sub is found in Parent then print 0
notes: Convert both strings to the same case before matching.
The archived solution counts overlapping matches.
If sub is longer than parent, the answer is 0.

title: Dictionary Subsequence Filter
match_hints: given a dictionary of N words and a master word
formed by deleting some characters of the given master word
in the order that the words appear in the dictionary
master word
notes: A valid dictionary word must be a subsequence of the master word.
The archived solution uses character positions plus binary search.
If no word matches, print -1.

title: Palindromic Sales List
match_hints: salesData
palindromicSalesList
add together any two consecutive elements
convert the given list into a palindromic list of maximum length
notes: Maximum length is equivalent to minimum number of merges.
Merge the side with the smaller running sum until both sides are equal.

title: Top-Left Submatrix Product Count
match_hints: contain the top left element of matrix A
product less than or equal to K
number of non-empty submatrices
submatrices B[1..X][1..Y]
notes: Only prefix rectangles need to be considered.
Any prefix rectangle containing zero has product 0 and is valid when K >= 0.
The archived solution caps large products at K + 1.

title: Minimum Cable Length
match_hints: systems are placed in a row
ON (1) or OFF (0)
minimum length of the cable
distance of the systems from the first system
connect everything except the largest adjacent gap
systemState_size
dist_size
notes: OFF systems before the first ON and after the last ON form forced chains.
Between two ON systems, the optimal cost is segment span minus the largest adjacent gap.
The archived parser accepts both the older 3-line input and the newer 4-line input with dist_size.

title: Smallest Registration Permutation
match_hints: car racing game
registration number
smallest permutation
never starts with zero
sample_output: smallest digit permutation without leading zero
notes: Positive numbers use ascending order with the first non-zero digit moved to the front.
Negative numbers use descending order on the absolute digits, then restore the negative sign.

title: Remove Duplicates Preserve Order
match_hints: remove all the duplicate numbers
contains only distinct numbers
same order as they appear in the input list
notes: Keep the first occurrence of each value.
Use a set for O(1) duplicate checks.

title: LRU Cache Misses
match_hints: Least Recently Used (LRU) cache
number of cache misses
virtual memory management system
page requests
notes: Use recency order to simulate LRU.
On a miss with full cache, evict the least recently used page.

title: Remove Vowels From String
match_hints: the vowels of the english alphabet are a e i o u A E I O U
write an algorithm to eliminate all vowels from a given string
print a string that excludes all the vowels of the given input string
the input string contains english letters only
in a town the houses are marked with english letters
renovate only the houses marked with vowels
identify the list of houses that will not be renovated
notes: Remove both lowercase and uppercase vowels.
Keep all remaining characters in their original order.
The same problem also appears with a houses-and-renovation story wrapper.

title: Maximum Meeting Attendees
match_hints: N number of CEOs will be attending
invitation ID from 0 to N-1
favorite CEO whom they like
seated next to the person they like
maximum number of CEOs who will attend the meeting
notes: Take the maximum of the largest cycle and the total contribution of all 2-cycles with their best incoming chains.
Use indegree pruning to compute the longest chain reaching each node outside cycles.

title: Perfect Number Check
match_hints: a perfect number is a number which is equal to the sum of all its factors
check whether N is a perfect number or not
print 1 if the given number is a perfect number otherwise print 0
notes: Proper divisors exclude the number itself.
0 and 1 are not perfect numbers.
Check divisors in pairs up to sqrt(N) for O(sqrt(N)) time.

title: Alternate Sort Of List
match_hints: alternate sort of a list
alternate elements starting from the first position
after sorting it in an ascending order
print space-separated integers representing the alternately sorted elements
notes: Sort ascending first, then keep indices 0, 2, 4, ...
The Java and Python screenshots are the same problem statement.
The archived solution reads N, then the next N integers.

title: Connected City Clusters
match_hints: city clusters
cities are connected by a network of roads
roads are bi-directional
total number of connected clusters
entry i j of the matrix is 1 if the ith city connects to the jth city
notes: Treat the matrix as an undirected graph adjacency matrix.
The answer is the number of connected components.
The archived parser accepts the screenshot format: row count, column count, then matrix rows.

title: Area Of Intersection Of Two Circles
match_hints: finding the area of intersection of two circles
x1 y1 r1 represents the x and y coordinates of the center of the first circle and the radius of the first circle
x2 y2 r2 represents the x and y coordinates of the center of the second circle and the radius of the second circle
print a real number representing the area of intersection of two circles rounded up to 6 decimal places
notes: If the circles do not overlap, the area is 0.
If one circle lies completely inside the other, the area is the area of the smaller circle.
Otherwise use the two-sector minus two-triangle lens-area formula.

title: Maximum Internship Salary
match_hints: stephen is doing an internship in a company for N days
each day he may choose either an easy task or a difficult task or perform no task at all
he can only choose a difficult task on the first day or when he did not perform any work on the previous day
calculate the maximum salary he can get
notes: Track three states after each day: skip, take easy, and take hard.
A hard task can only follow the skip state from the previous day.
The answer is the maximum value among the three end states.

title: Minimum Straight Line Pickup Routes
match_hints: transportation service in the city of nazeriana
identify the straight line routes starting from the base location
the number of routes are minimized and all the pickup locations are covered
first line contains N X0 Y0 and the next N lines contain Xi Yi
notes: Each route is a full straight line through the base location.
Pickup points that lie on the same line through the base share one route even if they are on opposite sides.
Normalize each direction vector by gcd and by folding opposite directions together.

title: Minimum Juice Stalls To Reach School
match_hints: john misses his bus and has to walk all his way from home to school
there are N juice stalls and each stall has a specific amount of juice in liters
his energy decreases by 1 unit for every unit of distance walked and increases by 1 for every liter of juice he consumes
find the minimum number of juice stalls at which he should stop to successfully reach the school or output -1
notes: This follows the minimum refueling stops greedy pattern.
Use a max-heap containing juice amounts from all already reachable stalls.
Whenever John cannot reach the next checkpoint, consume juice from the best previously reachable stall.
A visible predefined case with distances 10 20 22 23 26, juice 10 2 5 1 1, and D K = 30 10 returns -1.

title: Kth Soldier After Range Reversals
match_hints: there are N soldiers standing in a line and their personal IDs are from 1 to N
the major calls two numbers Li and Ri and soldiers swap in pairs until the segment is reversed
after Q action rounds find the ID of the soldier at the Kth position
input starts with N Q K followed by Q lines of Li Ri
soldiers stand at Li and Ri swap their positions
the process continues until Li plus m is greater than Ri minus m
the next Q lines consist of two space-separated integers Li and Ri representing the positions of the two soldiers
there are N soldiers standing in a line with IDs from 1 to N
each action round gives Li and Ri and reverses the whole segment from Li to Ri
after Q rounds find the ID of the soldier at the Kth position
notes: Each operation reverses the whole interval [L, R].
Instead of simulating the full permutation, track the target position backward through the operations.
If K lies inside [L, R], the previous position was L + R - K.
A visible predefined case with input 10 2 10 / 5 9 / 2 3 returns 10.
